description: A required quantum gate is absent from the circuit
real_bug_source: Bugs4Q; Empirical Quantum Platform Bug Study
expected_effect: Incorrect circuit transformation and wrong output distribution
research_notes: Strong baseline bug class for quantum mutation testing

description: An unintended quantum gate is added to the circuit
real_bug_source: Bugs4Q; Empirical Quantum Platform Bug Study
expected_effect: Unexpected state transformation and changed measurement probabilities
research_notes: Strong baseline bug class for artificial mutation datasets

description: A quantum gate is replaced with an incorrect gate
real_bug_source: Bugs4Q; Qiskit Bug Pattern Research; Empirical Quantum Platform Bug Study
expected_effect: Incorrect quantum operation causes wrong output state or distribution
research_notes: One of the most directly supported bug classes across mutation tools

description: A gate is applied to the wrong qubit or qubit pair
expected_effect: Correct operation affects the wrong quantum state component
research_notes: Very important for circuit correctness because qubit identity matters

description: Qubits are ordered or mapped incorrectly
expected_effect: Output interpretation or circuit semantics are reversed or misaligned
research_notes: Important real bug class but only partially captured by current mutation tools

description: The program assumes or initializes the wrong number of qubits
real_bug_source: Qiskit Bug Pattern Research; Empirical Quantum Platform Bug Study
expected_effect: Invalid circuit construction or incorrect algorithm size
research_notes: Useful for static analysis and future mutation operators

description: A required measurement operation is missing
expected_effect: No classical output is produced for required quantum state
research_notes: Directly supported by QMutPy and highly quantum-specific

description: A measurement is inserted too early or unnecessarily
expected_effect: Quantum state collapses before the algorithm finishes
research_notes: Important because measurement changes program semantics

description: A qubit is measured repeatedly or too many measurements are performed
expected_effect: May cause simulator errors backend issues or invalid output interpretation
research_notes: Good candidate for a new measurement repetition mutation operator

description: A quantum operation is incorrectly applied after measurement
expected_effect: Program applies quantum logic after collapse in an invalid or unintended way
research_notes: Can be partially simulated with measurement insertion plus gate mutation

description: An inverse or cleanup operation is applied incorrectly and leaves invalid state
expected_effect: Ancilla or intermediate state is not restored correctly
research_notes: Important algorithm-level bug not covered by simple gate mutation

description: A rotation angle or gate parameter value is incorrect
real_bug_source: Bugs4Q; QuanForge; Empirical Quantum Platform Bug Study
expected_effect: Incorrect rotation angle changes amplitudes and output probabilities
research_notes: Important for parameterized circuits and variational algorithms

description: A gate parameter has the wrong sign
expected_effect: Rotation direction is reversed and circuit behavior changes
research_notes: Especially useful for QNN and variational quantum algorithm testing

description: A parameter is assigned to the wrong gate or position
expected_effect: Learned or configured parameter affects the wrong operation
research_notes: Important for QNN robustness and trained circuit correctness

description: A correct gate is placed in the wrong circuit position
expected_effect: Non-commuting gates execute in the wrong order and produce wrong output
research_notes: Important because quantum operations are generally order-sensitive

description: A control qubit is added removed or assigned incorrectly
expected_effect: Entanglement or conditional operation is incorrect
research_notes: Important entanglement-related bug class

description: A custom gate is invalid or not recognized by the framework
expected_effect: Program fails during circuit construction transpilation or execution
research_notes: More API-specific than circuit-semantic but still relevant

description: The number of classical bits does not match measured qubits
expected_effect: Measurement results cannot be stored or interpreted correctly
research_notes: Strong static-analysis bug class and good future mutation target

description: A circuit or internal representation drops or stores incorrect information
expected_effect: Compiler or simulator uses an incorrect representation of the quantum program
research_notes: Important platform-level bug not covered by circuit mutation tools

description: The program uses the wrong bit ordering or endian convention
expected_effect: Output bits are interpreted in the wrong order
research_notes: Very important and currently under-supported by mutation tools

description: Quantum operations are scheduled at incorrect times
expected_effect: Low-level quantum instructions execute at the wrong time
research_notes: Important for hardware and compiler-level testing

description: A formula probability amplitude matrix or numerical computation is incorrect
expected_effect: Simulation or algorithm calculation produces incorrect results
research_notes: Partly classical but important for quantum simulators

description: Random seed sampling or probability behavior is handled incorrectly
expected_effect: Sampling results are unstable biased or unreproducible
research_notes: Relevant to quantum measurement sampling but not purely quantum-specific

description: The program uses the quantum framework API incorrectly
expected_effect: Program fails or behaves incorrectly due to incorrect API use
research_notes: Common but partly general software engineering

description: A program works on one backend or simulator but fails on another
real_bug_source: Bugs4Q; Empirical Quantum Platform Bug Study
expected_effect: Different execution targets produce failures or inconsistent behavior
research_notes: Important real-world issue but difficult to model with simple mutation
